and nearly as many of the apple
extra were destroyed, while both
engines were crushed into unrecog-
nizable heaps of scrap iron.


The bodies of Glass, engineer OF
the apple extra, Joe Keeie, condCc-
tor of 451. and Mcintyre, brake.
man on the head end of the apple
extra were covered by the debris,
which caught fire Immediately aft.
err the wreck, and up to this morn
ing had not yet been recovered


The three unkrCwns whose char
red bodies were taken from the
wreck, were "hobos" who had been
riding on the train, and it is be-
lieved probable that before the
wreck is cleared away one or more
bodies will be found, there having
been a number of unauthorized pas
sengers on train No. 451 at the time
of the smash. a-.


Officials Won't Talk.


Officials of the Great Northern
are reticent regarding the wreck
and it's cause today, refusing to
give out any information other than
to say that every effort is being
made to clear the track and to re-
cover the bodies of the victims
known to be under the debris.
